column37: Appraisers leave with a clearer understanding of why appraisers matter more — not less — in an AI-driven world, and how professional judgment, context, and accountability remain the true differentiators when data is abundant.

column2: The 2026 Housing Market So Far: Volume, Home Price and Supply Trends, and Appraisal Waivers Usage
column3:
As the housing market moves into 2026, valuation professionals face shifting transaction volume, evolving price dynamics, regional divergence, constrained supply, and continued growth in appraisal waiver usage.

This data-driven market update will provide a grounded analysis of national and regional housing trends, exploring what is changing, what is stabilizing, and what may present new risk or opportunity for valuation professionals.

Rather than speculation, this session focuses on measurable indicators and structural trends that will shape appraisal demand, workflow, and risk exposure in the year ahead.
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Review Appraisers, AMC Personnel, Collateral Risk Professionals

Mr. Peter has coauthored a variety of reports on housing policy, specifically on the impact of federal policy on housing demand and homeownership, housing finance risks, and first-time home buyers. He has testified before Congress, and his pieces have been published in policy journals and in the popular press, including in the Wall Street Journal, American Banker, and Business Insider.

He has a master’s in public policy from the Harvard Kennedy School and a bachelor’s degree in history and applied economics from the College of St. Scholastica.
column37: Attendees will leave with a clear, data-informed understanding of where the housing market is heading in 2026 — and what those trends mean for appraisal volume, pricing risk, waiver dynamics, and professional positioning in the year ahead.

column2: Computer Vision and Real Estate Appraisal: Practical Applications and Future Impact
column3:
This session introduces valuation professionals to computer vision as a practical, emerging component of modern appraisal workflows — moving beyond theory to real-world application.

As image-based data, property photos, and visual inspection inputs become more central to valuation processes, computer vision technologies are increasingly used to extract structured insight from unstructured visual information. This session focuses on how these tools are being applied today, where they add value, and how they may reshape appraisal processes over time.

The goal is to help appraisers understand computer vision as a supporting capability — not a replacement — and to think clearly about its role in quality, efficiency, and risk management.

As President of Valuations at Restb, Tony Pistilli is responsible for providing direction to the application of Restb.ai's products and services for the valuation segment of the real estate industry, working with the product team to develop and expand the suite of offerings and prioritizing development initiatives. Tony also plays a vital role in expanding Restb.ai's reach in the valuation and appraisal industry, as well as fostering relationships with lenders and related industry partners.  

Tony has over 30 years of executive-level real estate valuation and lending experience including working with national banks, mortgage companies, federal agencies, and leading appraisal management firms. He is a certified residential real estate appraiser in Texas and is an AQB Certified USPAP Instructor. In 2011, he was the first recipient of the Valuation Visionary Award presented by the Collateral Risk Network at Valuation Expo.
column37: Attendees leave with a clear understanding of how computer vision is being used in real estate appraisal today — where it adds value, where its limits remain, and how it is likely to influence appraisal quality, review, and risk management in the years ahead.

column2: How the Next Generation of Appraisers Is Actually Innovating
column3:
This session showcases real, working examples of how modern appraisers are innovating inside their practices — not by abandoning standards, but by rethinking how work gets done, how services are positioned, and how time and judgment are protected.

Rather than abstract conversations about “the future,” attendees will hear directly from appraisers who are actively using technology, refined workflows, and strategic positioning to work more efficiently, differentiate their services, and build sustainable careers in today’s market.

The goal is to replace vague innovation talk with practical, replicable ideas.
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Aspiring Appraisers, Review Appraisers, AMC Personnel, Collateral Risk Professionals
column22: Appraisal Brothers, Co-Owner / Certified Residential Appraiser
column23:
As Co-Owner and Certified Residential Appraiser at Appraisal Brothers, Victor Cornejo brings a dedicated career spanning over ten years in the real estate industry. His strong academic foundation includes both a Bachelor's in Political Science and a Master's in Legal Studies.

Victor strengthened his appraisal skills in the DFW area under the direct mentorship of a Certified General Appraiser, establishing him as an expert in the local market. His expertise extends to assisting a diverse clientele, providing essential services like home measurements, pre-listing appraisals, and pre-purchase appraisals for various needs, including those of agents, investors, and attorneys.

Victor is specifically responsible for overseeing complex appraisal assignments and ensuring the delivery of highest quality services to all Appraisal Brothers' clients.
column30:
MNAA – Member, National Association of Appraisers (NAA)

Member, Association of Texas Appraisers (ATA)

Member, National Association of Appraisers (NAA)
column37: Attendees leave with a practical, field-tested understanding of how modern appraisers are innovating inside their own practices — using technology, workflow redesign, and intentional service positioning to reduce friction, protect professional judgment, and differentiate beyond price, all while maintaining standards, compliance, and defensibility.

column2: Reconsideration of Value: Making the Strongest Case Possible
column3:
This session provides appraisers with a clear, repeatable framework for handling Reconsiderations of Value (ROVs) in today’s lending environment—without becoming reactive, compromising independence, or increasing compliance risk.

Drawing on more than 30 years of experience across appraisal practice, AMC leadership, and compliance oversight, Bill explains how ROVs are evaluated behind the scenes, what reviewers and lenders actually look for, and how appraisers can present the strongest possible case when new information is submitted.

The goal is to help appraisers treat ROVs as a professional judgment exercise, not a pressure event.
column23:
William "Bill" Waltenbaugh is a Certified Residential Appraiser with over 35 years in the property valuation industry. In his early years, he built a reputation for delivering exceptional valuation services to a diverse clientele.

In 2010, Bill advanced his career by taking on pivotal roles as the Chief Appraiser for multiple Appraisal Management Companies (AMCs) and a national lending institution. In these capacities, he was instrumental in steering the firms toward excellence by overseeing functions ranging from quality assurance and vendor management to rigorous compliance with industry regulations.

Currently, Bill serves as Chief Appraiser at Nationwide Appraisal Network (NAN), where he continues to leverage his vast experience and deep knowledge to advance NAN's commitment to technology, communication, and accountability. 

Looking ahead, Bill is passionate about the evolution of the valuation industry, particularly in product development and modernization. He is enthusiastically open to forming synergistic partnerships with like-minded professionals who share his vision for driving innovation and elevating industry standards.
column30:
Appraisal Institute (AI) - SRA, AI-RRS
National Association of Appraisers (NAA) - MNAA
New York State Board of Real Estate Appraisal - Current Board Member
column37: Real Estate Agents leave with a clear, reviewer-informed framework for handling Reconsiderations of Value — understanding how ROVs are actually evaluated, how to assess new information objectively, and how to respond with documentation and professional judgment that protects credibility and reduces repeat scrutiny.

column2: Freedom Appraise Demo: Appraiser-Built Software for End-to-End Workflow
column3:
This demo shows how Freedom Appraise is used as a mobile-first, cloud-based appraisal platform built by an appraiser to streamline the entire workflow—from data import and sketching through adjustments and report delivery.

The goal is to give attendees a realistic view of how the platform supports speed, flexibility, and automation while keeping control and judgment firmly with the appraiser.
column23: Andy Arledge is a real estate professional, appraiser, entrepreneur, and software innovator with more than 40 years of experience in sales, brokerage, property management, development, building, and investment. He is the President and Founder of Freedom Appraise LLC, creator of appraisal technology solutions including Appraiser Genie and Freedom Appraise, the first cloud-based appraisal software platform in the United States. He also leads Arledge Appraisals, LLC and AutoDealsAI LLC, and previously led Wrangler Builders, Inc. and Mr. G’s Burgers, reflecting a long track record of business leadership across real estate, technology, construction, and operations.
column37: Real Estate Appraisers leave with a clear understanding of how Freedom Appraise supports faster, more flexible appraisal workflows by automating mechanics while leaving valuation decisions to the appraiser.

column2: PAREA Explained: A Practical Update from the Appraisal Institute
column3:
This session provides a clear, practical explanation of PAREA (Practical Applications of Real Estate Appraisal) — what it is, why it exists, and how it fits into the future of appraisal education and entry pathways.

PAREA is often discussed in abstract or regulatory terms, leaving many appraisers unsure how it actually works or what it means for trainees, mentors, firms, and the profession as a whole. This session replaces speculation with clarity, explaining PAREA in plain language and outlining its real-world implications.

The goal is to help attendees understand PAREA as a tool, not a shortcut — and how it complements traditional mentorship and experience models.
column23: Appraisal Institute is a leading global professional association of real estate appraisers, dedicated to advancing valuation professionalism through education, advocacy, research, and ethical standards. The organization supports appraisers with specialized training, professional development, and respected designations that promote excellence and credibility across the valuation industry. Through its work, the Appraisal Institute helps strengthen public trust in real estate appraisal and supports informed decision-making in real estate markets.
column37: Aspiring Appraisers leave with a clear, practical understanding of what PAREA is, how it works, and how it may fit into potential education and entry pathways within the appraisal profession.

column2: Walitt Power Hour
column3:
Walitt Power Hour is intended to create a more interactive conference experience by giving attendees direct access to additional conversation with speakers, hosts, and subject matter experts outside the limits of a formal presentation slot. 

The goal is to provide a practical, informal setting where important ideas can be explored further, questions can be addressed in real time, and attendees can engage more directly with the people shaping the discussion.
column12: Real estate appraisers, Aspiring appraisers, Review appraisers, AMC personnel, Collateral risk professionals, Real estate agents, Lenders and other valuation-related decision-makers
column23: Real estate professional and leadership coach with deep experience across mortgage, appraisal, and valuation services. At Walitt Solutions, she helps organizations strengthen compliance, training, and team alignment through practical, people-centered guidance.
column37: Attendees leave with deeper context, clearer perspective, and more direct access to the people and ideas shaping the future of valuation.

column2: A la mode Demo: Modern Appraisal Reporting in Practice
column3:
This demo shows how modern appraisal reporting software is actually used in day-to-day practice—from assignment setup through report delivery—highlighting workflow structure, consistency, and review readiness in real production environments.

Rather than showcasing features, the focus is on how appraisers work inside the platform: how tasks flow, where time is saved, and how reporting decisions are supported in a compliant, defensible way.
column23: Joel Baker is a longtime appraisal-technology product leader at a la mode technologies, Inc., a Cotality company, where he serves as Senior Professional, Product. With more than two decades at the company, he has helped shape appraisal software and workflow solutions used across the industry, serving as a strong advocate for appraisers and translating real-world practice into product improvements, training, and support. Baker is also a respected industry educator and speaker, known for leading webinars, conference sessions, and discussions on major industry changes, including UAD 3.6 and the redesigned URAR, helping appraisers prepare for what’s next.
column25: a la mode technologies, inc., a Cotality company
column37: Real Estate Appraisers leave with a clear, realistic understanding of how modern appraisal reporting software is used in real production—how workflows are structured, where efficiency is gained, and how consistent, review-ready reports are built day to day.

column2: Best Practices for As-Is and As-Repaired Valuations of REO Assets
column3:
This session focuses on best practices for developing credible as-is and as-repaired valuations for REO properties, where value conclusions directly inform asset management, repair strategy, pricing, and disposition decisions.

Rather than discussing standardized form requirements, the session centers on how lenders and asset managers evaluate appraisal logic, repair assumptions, and market support in REO assignments — and where appraisers most often create clarity, or unintended risk, through their analysis and commentary.
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Review Appraisers, AMC Personnel, Collateral Risk Professionals
column23: Mike Scarpa is a certified appraiser and valuation executive with nearly three decades of industry experience. He began his career in 1996 as a field appraiser in Salt Lake City, training alongside his father and brothers, and later spent 14 years appraising in Arizona before joining Fannie Mae in 2010. There, he advanced through a series of leadership and training roles across multiple review teams and projects. Today, Mike serves as SVP, Head of Valuation Oversight at Freedom Mortgage, where he leads oversight of property valuation controls, quality, compliance, policy, technology, appraisal industry government affairs, and third-party valuation risk. He is also a recognized subject matter expert in valuations and holds a degree in Organizational Leadership from Arizona State University, along with a certificate in Applied Data Analytics.
column37: Attendees leave with a practical understanding of how as-is and as-repaired REO valuations are reviewed and relied upon by lenders and asset managers, and what best practices help their reports remain clear, supportable, and defensible throughout the REO lifecycle — from repair decisions through final disposition.

column2: A Career in Business Valuation
column3:
This session introduces business valuation as a profession and career path through the lens of a practitioner who has spent decades doing the work—and then stepped back to help others build successful valuation practices.

Business valuation is often encountered indirectly by appraisers, accountants, or analysts, but few understand what the day-to-day work actually looks like or how professionals enter the field. This session explains the discipline in plain terms, traces a realistic career path into business valuation, and shows how the profession evolves over time.

The goal is to help attendees understand whether business valuation is a viable and attractive career—and what it truly takes to succeed.
column23: Rod Burkert, BVFLS is a business valuation professional and practice-management coach who helps BVFLS professionals and firms turn the practices they have into the practices they want—now with an added focus on practical AI support for the valuation profession. He has been performing business valuations in various capacities since the late 1980s, and in 2000 he founded Burkert Valuation Advisors in Philadelphia, running a traditional valuation practice with a specialization in tax-purpose valuations for manufacturers and distributors. From 2010 to 2022, Rod and his wife traveled full-time throughout the U.S. and Canada, an experience that shaped his perspective on building a location-independent practice and led him to coaching other BVFLS firms. Now based in Bisbee, Arizona, he focuses on helping valuation professionals strengthen operations and practice management, and since late 2022 he has also been exploring how AI can support BVFLS work—resulting in his AI for BV™ newsletter and AI implementation services designed specifically for valuation practices.
column37: Aspiring Appraisers leave with a clear, realistic understanding of what a career in business valuation actually involves—how the work is practiced day to day, how professionals enter the field, and whether this path aligns with their skills, interests, and long-term goals.

column2: The Fundamental Purpose of Appraisal: Redefining Risk in a Modern Economy
column3:
This high-level panel brings together senior leadership from Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to address a fundamental question: What is the true purpose of the mortgage appraisal in today’s landscape? By consolidating these two powerhouse perspectives, this session clarifies how the role of the appraisal is evolving from a static requirement into a dynamic component of risk management.

Participants will gain a unified vision from the GSEs on how collateral policy and valuation standards are aligning to interpret market realities, ensuring the appraisal process remains the anchor for safety and soundness in the housing finance system.
column23:
Lyle Radke is a residential valuation expert who specializes in applying appraisal theory to complex, real-world problems to create scalable industry solutions. 

He currently serves as the Senior Director of Collateral Policy at Fannie Mae, where he leads the team responsible for policies related to property value, property eligibility, and project eligibility. In this role, he oversees the development of policy standards through the Selling Guide and the Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD), while supporting the profession through education and outreach programs like the Appraiser Diversity Initiative (ADI).

With over 30 years of experience in the industry, his professional background includes:Executive Leadership: 

Served as Chief Appraiser for both Altisource and Ocwen Financial Corporation.

Valuation Management: Managed servicing valuation and collateral risk teams at GMAC ResCap.

Field Experience: Spent over eight years as a Fee Appraiser, building a reputation for excellence in complex and high-end residential appraisals.

Lyle is dedicated to appraisal modernization and the implementation of new ideas through collaboration with technology teams, lenders, and regulators. 

He holds a Master of Science in Geology from Brigham Young University and is fluent in Spanish.
column24: Senior Director of Collateral Policy, Fannie Mae
column37: Attendees will leave with a profound understanding of the appraisal’s renewed purpose and how the GSE risk framework is evolving. This session provides the strategic foundation necessary for appraisers, lenders, and risk managers to anticipate shifts in collateral oversight and housing finance strategy for the years ahead.

column2: The Fundamental Purpose of Appraisal: Redefining Risk in a Modern Economy
column3:
This high-level panel brings together senior leadership from Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to address a fundamental question: What is the true purpose of the mortgage appraisal in today’s landscape? By consolidating these two powerhouse perspectives, this session clarifies how the role of the appraisal is evolving from a static requirement into a dynamic component of risk management.

Participants will gain a unified vision from the GSEs on how collateral policy and valuation standards are aligning to interpret market realities, ensuring the appraisal process remains the anchor for safety and soundness in the housing finance system.
column23:
Scott Reuter is the Single-Family Chief Appraiser and Director of Valuation for Freddie Mac. He is a Certified-General Appraiser with 30+ years of experience in valuation, appraisal and collateral risk management concerns.

He holds a B.A. from The Ohio State University, and has completed the Executive Leadership program at Cornell University. His management experience includes positions with Bank of America, GE and Goldman Sachs. Scott is a second generation appraiser and began his career as a practicing fee appraiser.

His team leads the effort for ongoing development and refinement of property valuation risk management strategies, underwriting products, and establishing and maintaining a credible quality assurance process across multiple lines of business support.
column37: Attendees will leave with a profound understanding of the appraisal’s renewed purpose and how the GSE risk framework is evolving. This session provides the strategic foundation necessary for appraisers, lenders, and risk managers to anticipate shifts in collateral oversight and housing finance strategy for the years ahead.

column2: Cultivating the Next Generation: Success and Impact of the Appraiser Development Initiative (ADI)
column3: The appraisal profession faces a critical need to refresh its talent and evolve professionally. This session explores the Appraiser Development Initiative (ADI), a cornerstone program designed to reduce barriers to entry and provide comprehensive education for aspiring appraisers. Rachel Beam will share the strategic vision behind ADI, its measurable impact on industry growth, and how fostering a robust talent pipeline strengthens the accuracy and reliability of the entire valuation ecosystem.
column23: Rachel Beam is the Director of Valuations at Fannie Mae, a position she has held since October 2019. With over 13 years of leadership at Fannie Mae, she has overseen critical areas including QC Field Appraisal Management for the Western US and valuation quality control standards. Prior to her tenure at Fannie Mae, she spent nearly 17 years as a Region Appraisal Manager at World Savings/Wachovia. She is a recognized industry expert with a specialized focus on appraisals, real estate, and REO management.
column37: Attendees will gain a clear understanding of how the Appraiser Development Initiative is practically reshaping the profession. They will leave with actionable ideas on how to foster professional growth within their own organizations and a deeper appreciation for why a robust development pipeline is essential for the future of valuation risk management.

column2: The New Era of Appraisal Data: Navigating the UAD Redesign
column3: The Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) is undergoing its most significant evolution in years. Originally slated for a niche track, this session has been elevated to the Main Stage because the UAD redesign is not just a technical update—it is the foundation of the future of valuation. Ken DeFeo will break down how this shift to a more flexible, data-driven framework will eliminate legacy friction, improve reporting quality, and create a scalable ecosystem for appraisers, lenders, and real estate professionals alike.
column23:
Ken DeFeo is a Credit Risk Manager at Fannie Mae, where he has served for nearly seven years within the Washington D.C. Metro Area. An expert in executive operations and process improvement, Ken specializes in streamlining complex systems and implementing technology to increase productivity and quality without increasing headcount.

With a career spanning over three decades in the mortgage and valuation sectors, his experience includes:

Executive Leadership: Served as Director at Union Bank, where he oversaw all residential appraisal operations for the financial holding company.

Operational Integration: As VP - Operations Manager II at Chase Home Finance, he led the high-paced integration of legacy organizations (Chase, WaMu, and Bear Stearns), focusing on vendor standardization and the startup of Quality Control departments.

Long-term Strategy: Spent 12 years as SVP, Chief Appraiser, and Operations Lead at BNC Mortgage (a Lehman Brothers Company), driving revenue growth and operational efficiency through diverse market cycles.

Ken is recognized for his "out-of-the-box" thinking and his ability to build scalable workflow systems that adapt to both rapid growth and economic retrenchment.
column24: Credit Risk Manager at Fannie Mae
column37: Participants will leave with a comprehensive understanding of the new UAD framework and a practical guide to the upcoming changes. Attendees will understand how to leverage these data improvements to increase their own operational efficiency and ensure long-term compliance in a modernized valuation landscape.

column2:
Using AI Beyond the Appraisal Process: Creating
Value Across the Valuation Workflow
column3:
This session is designed to help valuation professionals understand how AI can create value beyond the appraisal process itself. The goal is to give attendees a practical
view of how AI may support the broader workflow surrounding valuation, including coordination, communication, documentation, review support, and operational efficiency.
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Review Appraisers, AMC Personnel, Collateral Risk Professionals
column23: Narainder Chandwani is an engineering leader and technology entrepreneur with a background in software development, product strategy, and security. He is the Founder and CEO of apprAIz, where he focuses on building technology solutions for the appraisal industry, particularly around workflow efficiency and adapting to changes such as UAD 3.6. Before launching apprAIz, he held senior engineering leadership roles at Fanatics, Hotwire, Macy’s, and McAfee/Foundstone. He holds a master’s degree in computer science and information security from NYU Polytechnic School of Engineering and has also studied product management at Stanford University.
column37: Attendees leave with a clearer understanding of how AI can support the valuation workflow beyond the appraisal process, where caution is required, and why professional judgment remains essential as these tools continue to evolve.

column2:
Blockchain in Real Estate: From Crypto Closings to
On-Chain Title and What It Means for Valuation
column3:
This session is designed to help valuation professionals understand how blockchain, AI, and digitally enabled transaction models are beginning to influence real estate workflows. The goal is not to speculate, but to give attendees a practical view of
where these changes may affect title, closing, ownership transfer, and the valuation ecosystem around them.
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Review Appraisers, AMC Personnel, Collateral Risk Professionals
column23: Eric L. Cruz is the Chief Revenue Officer at Propy and a sales and proptech leader with deep experience in SaaS, AI, blockchain, and real estate. He is known for building high-performance sales systems, driving growth, and helping modernize the transaction experience through technology and strong leadership.
column37: Attendees leave with a clearer understanding of how blockchain and AI may reshape transaction workflows, where valuation professionals should pay attention, and why practical judgment remains essential as these models evolve.

column2: Expert Witness Foundations: What Appraisers Need to Know to Get Started
column3: This session gives appraisers a practical introduction to expert witness and litigation work, with a focus on the foundational elements that matter most when getting started. It is designed to help attendees understand how this work differs from traditional lender assignments, how to engage effectively with attorney clients, and how to build the structure, communication, and compensation framework needed before testimony is ever required.
column23: Kyle has been a Certified Residential Appraiser since 2015 and currently serves as the Education Committee Leader for the REAA East Bay (Real Estate Appraiser Association). In that role, Kyle has taught and developed continuing education courses on ADUs, appraiser training, and market analysis. Kyle became an Accredited Green Appraiser in 2023. Kyle is a proud member of the National Association of Appraisers (NAA) and the Community of Asset Analysts(CAA). He lives with his wife and two daughters in Benicia where he operates a mid-term rental ADU.
column37: Attendees leave with a practical understanding of how to get started in expert witness and litigation appraisal work — including the terminology, attorney communication, compensation structures, and foundational practices that matter most before testimony is ever required.

column2: Housing Policy, Affordability, and Market Risk:  What Valuation Professionals Should Be Watching
column3: To help valuation professionals make sense of the housing policy shifts shaping affordability, lending, and market risk—and to understand what those changes may signal for the future of valuation practice
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Equipment Appraisers, Review Appraisers, Aspiring Appraisers, Valuation Professionals
column23: Erin Dee is a mortgage operations executive and industry advocate serving as Chief Operating Officer at InterLinc Mortgage and President of the Texas Mortgage Bankers Association. With more than 15 years of experience in mortgage operations, technology, capital markets, and strategy, she is known for building scalable systems, strengthening teams, and advancing housing policy that supports communities and the broader mortgage industry.
column37: Housing policy is becoming a more direct driver of affordability, lending behavior, and market risk—and valuation professionals need to understand those connections to stay ahead of what’s next.

column2: Solar in UAD 3.6: What Appraisers Should Do Next
column3: This session is designed to help appraisers think through a practical UAD 3.6 scenario involving a property with solar panels and other energy-related features. Using a case-based approach, the session will focus on what appraisers should do next, what options are available to them, and how to approach the work in a way that is credible, practical, and useful in the field.
column23: Brent Bowen is a Certified Residential Appraiser and President/Chief Appraiser of Texas Valuation Professionals, Inc., a Plano-based residential appraisal and valuation consulting firm serving the Dallas–Fort Worth market. A Baylor University graduate with a background in real estate and economics, he has worked in North Texas real estate since 1997. In addition to his appraisal practice, Bowen is an active educator and industry contributor, known for developing courses and tools focused on market analysis, solar valuation, and Excel-based appraisal methods, while also serving as a Volunteer Reviewer for the Texas Appraiser Licensing and Certification Board.
column37: Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of how to approach a UAD 3.6 assignment involving solar, what questions to ask, what options are available, and how to move forward with greater confidence and structure.

column2: How to Build an Appraisal Business That Doesn’t Rely on Marketing
column3: This session is designed to help appraisers think more strategically about how to build a durable business without depending on constant marketing activity or waiting for lender volume to return. Drawing on practical experience across changing market cycles, the session will explore how appraisers can strengthen their business through positioning, specialization, diversification, and reputation-based work that creates more stability over time.
column12: Real Estate Appraisers, Review Appraisers, AMC Personnel, Collateral Risk Professionals
column23: Brent Henry, SRA, AI-RRS, is a Denver-area valuation professional and real estate broker serving as Chief Appraiser at Green Tree and owner of Green Tree Real Estate. With more than 20 years of experience in the Denver metro market, he specializes in residential appraisal and brings strong local knowledge to complex valuation assignments across the region.
column24: Chief Appraiser at Green Tree and Owner, Green Tree Real Estate
column25: Grenn Tree and Green Tree Real Estate
column37: Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of how to build a more durable appraisal business by relying less on marketing and more on specialization, diversification, reputation, and strategic positioning.

column2:
If I Were Starting Today: Real-World Guidance
for New Appraisers
column3: This session is designed to help aspiring appraisers and other early-career entrants think more clearly and practically about how to begin well in the profession. Drawing on real-world experience in training, mentoring, and appraiser development, the session will focus on the steps that matter most at the start: building a strong foundation, developing practical skills, understanding what the work really requires, and approaching the profession with more confidence and direction.
column12: Aspiring Appraisers, College Students, Military Veterans Exploring Valuation Careers
column23: Melissa Bond is a Certified Residential Appraiser licensed in Mississippi and Louisiana and the developer of the Mississippi Practical Appraiser Training program (MPAT), one of the first practicum-based training models created for aspiring appraisers. Since 1994, she has operated her own appraisal practice, serving clients that include VA, FHA, USDA, Fannie Mae, title companies, attorneys, school districts, and the general public. In addition to her appraisal work, Bond is a multi-state continuing education provider and course author who has developed more than 40 courses for appraisers and related industry professionals. Her leadership has also included service as a contract reviewer for state investigations, subject matter expert for licensing jurisdictions, and board member for several appraisal organizations, including the National Association of Appraisers and the Appraisal Standards Board.
column24: Developer of the Mississippi Practical Appraiser Training program (MPAT)
column37: Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of what they should focus on first if they are serious about becoming appraisers, and how to start building the habits, skills, and foundation needed for a strong career.

column2: UAD 3.6 for Agents: How to Read the New Reports
column3: This session is designed to help real estate agents understand how to read and interpret the new UAD 3.6 reports in a practical, non-technical way. Using selected sample reports, the session will highlight key changes in the new format and show where appraisers may request additional information from agents during the valuation process.
column23:
Pamela Teel, ATA-G, MNAA, is a State Certified General Real Estate Appraiser in the State of Texas, an AQB Certified USPAP Instructor, an IDECC Certified Distance Educator, and an NAA Certified Appraisal Reviewer. Her current appraisal practice consists of appraisal review, education, mentoring and consulting. Pam previously owned Corpus Christi Appraisal Service in Corpus Christi, Texas, and held a position as staff appraiser for a nationwide lender where she was responsible for development and delivery of appraisal policies and appraisal review training. Prior to becoming a real estate appraiser, Pam was the Vice President of Operations for a commercial property management company, managing office buildings, shopping centers, industrial properties, and apartment complexes.

Pam has been appointed by the State of Texas Appraiser Licensing and Certification Board as a Mentor, is past President of the Texas Association of Appraisers, and is currently Secretary of the National Association of Appraisers.
column37: Agents do not need to become appraisers, but they do need to know how to read the new UAD 3.6 reports, recognize the changes that matter, and understand where appraisers may need additional information or context from them.

column2: Market Moves with Mark and Jeff: AI in Modern Appraisal Practice
column3:
This live recording of Market Moves brings a trusted industry voice directly to the ValuSignal audience to
examine how AI is actually showing up in modern appraisal practice. The session focuses on practical use
cases, professional judgment, and where technology genuinely adds—or complicates—value in appraisal
workflows.
Rather than speculative futurism, this conversation centers on what appraisers are encountering now and
how leading practitioners are thinking about adoption, risk, and defensibility.
column23: Mark Verrett, CEO, leads a team of valuation professionals with the goal of changing and bettering the valuation industry. He is a second-generation appraiser focused on modernization and efficiencies in the real estate space. Previously, Mark served as the Chief Innovation Officer for Accurity. He has also been a director in a national real estate database company, leading the development of innovative software and applications in real estate. Mark was also selected to serve the Appraisal Institute nationally as third director of Region VIII. Mark’s extensive level of experience in the analysis of the appraisal process allows Accurity to provide a level of speed and efficiency to clients that is unmatched in the industry.
column30: SRA, ASA, IFA
column37: Attendees gain a grounded, practitioner-level understanding of where AI adds real value in appraisal practice—and where professional judgment, standards, and defensibility still matter most—so they can engage AI conversations with confidence, clarity, and credibility.

column2: The Future of Appraising and the Role of the ASA
column3:
This session examines how the appraisal profession is evolving—and the role the American Society of

Appraisers plays in helping professionals navigate that change. It is designed to give attendees a forward-
looking view of where appraisal practice is heading, how standards and expectations are shifting, and why

professional leadership and multidisciplinary perspective matter in the next phase of valuation.
column24: International President, American Society of Appraisers; President, Strategic Asset Management
column25: American Society of Appraisers, Strategic Asset Management
column37: Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of where the appraisal profession is heading, why professional organizations like the ASA continue to matter, and how appraisers can position themselves to stay credible and relevant as the industry evolves.
